highlights.text: On Tuesday at 4pm, I was looking for more, “My general lean is to defer to the trend.
I wrote at 4pm yesterday: “My general lean is always to defer to the trend until evidence tells me otherwise.

NOTE: This is a resend of the newsletter for today as the first contained some incorrect numbers in the trade plan (typos) The last 2.5 weeks have seen staggering upside in ES and has been the best trade in a few years.
Institutions are always positioning in advance, and my edge is to recognize the pattern which prints on the chart when they do so: My core setup - the Failed Breakdown.
As I frequently discuss all major rallies in ES start on Failed Breakdowns, because Failed Breakdowns are how institutions accumulate .
A flush and recovery of this low is a clear Failed Breakdown and a powerful one.” ES flushed to 6760’s Sunday evening, recovered 6780 shortly after (Failed Breakdown) and ripped.

I knew however that this run was just getting started because not only did we see that big Failed Breakdown Sunday, but readers recall last Tuesday, ES broke out from a month long bull flag (shown in white below).
In today’s newsletter I’ll expand on this, I’ll go over today’s Failed Breakdowns (these are key to know), and I’ll discuss the actionable plan for tomorrow
7016 and 6683 are key supports tomorrow if we finally retrace.” Today, ES spent the session mostly consolidating below 7075.

post_title: [RE-SEND] 4 Green Days This Week For SPX. Can It Close Up With 5? April 17 Plan
